name: False Kiva
description: This now-protected stone circle in Canyonlands is an archeological mystery and a stellar viewpoint.
_notes: Coords pulled from atlas-obscura JSON-LD on 2026-05-04 (verified via Playwright fetch before Cloudflare lock-out).

name: Mesa Arch
description: An iconic sunrise photography spot in Canyonlands' Island in the Sky district.
_notes: Approximate coords from external map references; confirm next time atlas-obscura is reachable.

name: Newspaper Rock Petroglyphs
description: A 200-square-foot rock panel covered with one of the largest concentrations of petroglyphs in North America, spanning 2,000 years of indigenous history.

name: Wilson Arch
description: A roadside arch right off US-191 south of Moab, easily climbed for sweeping La Sal views.

name: Fisher Towers
description: Towering 900-foot sandstone pillars that draw climbers and sunset photographers along the Colorado River north of Moab.

name: Crystal Geyser
description: A rare cold-water geyser on the Green River that erupts on an unpredictable schedule, formed by an abandoned 1930s drilling project.

name: Corona Arch
description: A massive sandstone arch reached by a moderate hike with cable and ladder sections — once notorious for rope-swinging.

name: Grandstaff Canyon (Morning Glory Bridge)
description: A shaded creek-side canyon hike outside Moab leading to the sixth-longest natural rock span in the United States.
